faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Hollis home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool, silk, or cashmere garments, webbing in closet corners, larvae (small cream-colored caterpillars) on fabrics, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice damage to carpets, upholstery, or stored blankets. If you spot any of these signs in your Hollis home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does the clothes moth control treatment process involve?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive treatment includes a detailed inspection to locate breeding sites, identification of the moth species, targeted application of safe insecticides to affected areas, treatment of closets and storage spaces, and recommendations for cleaning and prevention. We also provide guidance on proper garment storage and follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination of the infestation.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control pricing vary from $550 to $5,000 in Hollis?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, the number of affected areas requiring treatment, and whether follow-up visits are needed. A minor infestation in a single closet may cost around $550, while extensive infestations affecting multiple rooms, storage areas, and valuable items throughout a larger Hollis home may require more intensive treatment approaching $5,000.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets, storing woolens and natural fibers in sealed containers or garment bags, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, avoiding storing dirty clothes, and maintaining low humidity levels. We provide detailed prevention strategies specific to your Hollis home and offer ongoing monitoring services to catch any new activity early.
